description: PU-2 (ADR-017) — portable conformance vectors for the KEYSTONE context-carriage contract (common/v1/context.proto + ADR-007 gateway stamping). The most-irreversible frozen surface (the carrier for C1/C2/C3/C5/C7/C8) gets the ADR-003 two-reference forcing function it skipped at freeze: two technologically-divergent reference implementations (Go + Python) cross-run these vectors. Each impl is a pure reference of the contract a core gateway / consuming hop MUST apply on each hop: read the inbound RequestContext, REJECT an ill-formed envelope, else RE-STAMP a fresh downstream RequestContext (trace verbatim; identity server-controlled).

signing.canonical: principal \x00 tenant \x00 bound_correlation_id \x00 decimal(expires_unix_ms) \x00 key_id (NUL-separated; the bytes the core signs and a hop reconstructs FROM THE BARE MIRRORS to verify — so M4 is enforced structurally: a bare value the signature does not cover fails verification)

contract: C1: traceparent MUST be present + well-formed (00-<32hex>-<16hex>-<2hex>) and correlation_id MUST be present, else reject.

RE-STAMP: trace is propagated VERBATIM; identity.caller_plugin is RE-DERIVED from this hop's authenticated channel (channel_caller) and NEVER propagated from the inbound envelope; tenant is server-stamped (propagated); deadline propagated.

SUBJECT (if present): verify the ed25519 signature over the canonical bytes reconstructed from the BARE mirrors (SubjectAssertion.principal + Identity.tenant) + bound + expires + key_id by the key_id's key; bound_correlation_id MUST equal trace.correlation_id (anti-stockpile); now <= expires_unix_ms; M4 = reconstruct-from-bare, so a tampered bare principal/tenant fails the signature.
An absent subject is valid only for pre-auth/bootstrap; downstream subject_principal is empty.

cases.name: reject-missing-correlation-id
cases.desc: C1: well-formed traceparent but no correlation_id -> reject.

cases.name: accept-bootstrap-no-subject
cases.desc: Valid trace, no subject (pre-auth bootstrap): accept; caller re-derived, no subject principal downstream.

cases.name: restamp-caller-rederived-not-propagated
cases.desc: The inbound envelope CLAIMS caller_plugin=rat-evil; the gateway IGNORES it and re-derives from the authenticated channel (rat-format). This is the C3 cross-plugin-namespace guarantee.

cases.name: accept-valid-subject-trace-verbatim
cases.desc: A fully-valid signed subject: accept; trace propagated verbatim; subject principal carried downstream.

cases.name: reject-subject-bad-signature
cases.desc: Subject signature tampered -> reject (never trust principal without a valid signature).

cases.name: reject-subject-unknown-keyid
cases.desc: key_id not in the core keyring -> reject (no key to verify against).

cases.name: reject-subject-wrong-bound-correlation
cases.desc: bound_correlation_id != inbound correlation_id (a stockpiled assertion reused for another op) -> reject.

cases.name: reject-m4-tenant-mirror-mismatch
cases.desc: M4: signature was minted over tenant=tenantA, but the bare Identity.tenant is tenantB. Reconstructing the signed bytes from the bare mirror fails the signature -> reject. (A hop reading the bare tenant must not be handed a value the signature does not cover.)

cases.name: reject-m4-principal-mirror-mismatch
cases.desc: M4: signature was minted over principal=user-1, but the bare SubjectAssertion.principal is user-2. Reconstruct-from-bare fails the signature -> reject.
